paper.title: WiCompass: Oracle-driven Data Scaling for mmWave Human Pose Estimation
paper.abstract: Millimeter-wave Human Pose Estimation (mmWave HPE) promises privacy but suffers from poor generalization under distribution shifts. We demonstrate that brute-force data scaling is ineffective for out-of-distribution (OOD) robustness; efficiency and coverage are the true bottlenecks. To address this, we introduce WiCompass, a coverage-aware data-collection framework. WiCompass leverages large-scale motion-capture corpora to build a universal pose space ``oracle'' that quantifies dataset redundanc

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: Reduced memory usage by 30% after applying compression

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Prioritizing coverage-aware data acquisition can lead to significant improvements in OOD accuracy at matched budgets.
technical_details: Closed-loop policy for prioritizing informative missing samples
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Feasible on both mobile and laptop, but may require optimization for mobile due to limited RAM.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
